# RF-CBC-002 — Pediatric pancytopenia / marrow-failure scope-exit evidence

## Governance banner

> UNVALIDATED research prototype. rf output — a PROPOSAL, not a validated rule. No autonomous diagnosis/treatment/dosing/transfusion directives. No unsupported confidence %. Missingness never treated as normal. Every clinical threshold tied to an exact passage or flagged a proposal.

## Executive summary

This dossier assembles the evidence located for a pediatric CBC-interpretation module's handling of pancytopenia and multilineage marrow failure, scoped to scope-exit and referral logic rather than any autonomous clinical action. Acquired aplastic anemia incidence is 2-3 per million population per year in the Western world and about three times higher in East Asia. [claim:clm_037] Aplastic anemia incidence shows a biphasic age distribution with a first peak at 10-25 years (relevant to older children/adolescents) and a second peak over 60 years, with a European incidence of 2-3 per million per year. [claim:clm_066] Severe aplastic anemia (SAA) is a rare, potentially fatal hematologic disorder for which overall treatment outcomes are excellent, though management approaches vary and differ between adult and pediatric patients. [claim:clm_009] Certain aspects of pediatric SAA treatment remain under active investigation in clinical trials, so recommendations are not fully settled. [claim:clm_010] Because SAA is rare, some pediatric hematologists have relatively limited experience with its complex management. [claim:clm_011] Consolidated panel recommendations are framed as an up-to-date, evidence-based approach specifically for children with newly diagnosed SAA. [claim:clm_012]

A pediatric module carries a distinct pre-test prior. **Inference:** A pediatric CBC module carries a higher pre-test probability of an inherited (versus acquired) marrow-failure cause than an adult module and should weight IBMFS triggers accordingly: inherited-marrow-failure onset clusters in childhood — DC/TBD AR/XLR median diagnosis 11.3 y and TINF2 8.1 y, the IBMFS proteogenomic cohort median onset 3 y, and classic DBA 90% symptomatic within the first year of life. [claim:clm_inf10] The discovery cohort of 60 IBMFS patients had a median age at disease onset of 3 years (range 0-23) and included 27 men. [claim:clm_021] Classic DBA presents with macrocytic anaemia and reticulocytopenia, with 90% of patients becoming symptomatic in the first year of life at a median age of 3 months (though presentation can range from in-utero hydrops to the sixth decade). [claim:clm_048] Median age at diagnosis in telomere biology disorders differed sharply by inheritance mode: AD 36.1 years, AR/XLR 11.3 years, and TINF2 8.1 years. [claim:clm_035] SDS carries a 15-30% risk of progression to myelodysplastic syndrome and acute myeloid leukemia, motivating early identification. [claim:clm_023]

Counts alone do not separate the pediatric differential. In an observed RCC cohort, 84% (84/100) presented with a hypocellular diagnostic bone marrow, consistent with the typical RCC presentation. [claim:clm_001] That cohort's median age at diagnosis was 10.9 years (range 1.4-17.3), with slight male predominance (M/F 1.2) and age-adjusted MCV increased in ~half of patients. [claim:clm_008] For observed normal-karyotype RCC without predisposition, 5- and 10-year transplant(HSCT)-free survival were 94% and 88%, with all patients alive at median 7.2-year follow-up. [claim:clm_005] Patients who later underwent HSCT had lower median diagnosis-time Hb (8.7 vs 10.6 g/dL) and platelets (30 vs 64 ×10^9/L) than those never transplanted. [claim:clm_007] A protocolized in-depth evaluation established a causative diagnosis in 40% (20/50) of pediatric BMF patients, 18 of them by genetic analysis (14 mutations, 4 chromosomal deletions). [claim:clm_071] The authors of that protocol recommend full genome-wide genetic analysis (WES with CNV plus SNP-array) complemented by functional tests in all suspected BMF patients, because causative genetic defects occur even without typical syndromic features. [claim:clm_073] Of 100 children with suspected FA, 13 (13%) had a true FA cellular phenotype, 27 had possible mosaic FA, and the remainder were classified non-FA. [claim:clm_026]

## Scope-exit / referral triggers

Each trigger below — a source-attributed statement or a labeled proposal, never an autonomous in-line module action.

- **Diagnostic-construct boundary.** Acquired aplastic anemia is defined as pancytopenia with a hypoplastic bone marrow, framing the diagnostic construct that a CBC module cannot close on counts alone. [claim:clm_038]
- **Marrow biopsy required.** The IAP consensus states a bone marrow biopsy is mandatory to establish a diagnosis of acquired aplastic anemia in children. [claim:clm_013]
- **Mandatory exclusions before an acquired label.** Before diagnosing acquired AA, the consensus requires excluding inherited bone marrow failure syndromes, connective tissue disorders, viral infections, PNH, and drug/heavy-metal marrow suppression in all cases. [claim:clm_014]
- **Reflex IBMF testing by age band.** For IBMF exclusion, patients aged <=40 years (encompassing the entire pediatric band) are recommended to have chromosome breakage analysis (Fanconi anemia) and telomere length analysis; age 41-60 case-by-case; >60 with prior normal counts not recommended. [claim:clm_059]
- **Malignancy-first ordering.** The protocol's first diagnostic step is exclusion of malignant and transient/reversible causes of BMF — patients are screened for leukemia and referred to a pediatric oncology center if indicated — before extended genetic/functional workup begins. [claim:clm_072]
- **RCC scope-exit criteria.** EWOG-MDS assigned early HSCT (scope-exit from observation) for RCC patients with transfusion dependency, ANC <1×10^9/L, monosomy 7, del(7q), or >=2 chromosomal aberrations; others were observed. [claim:clm_002]
- **First-line functional screens are non-terminal.** The MMC sensitivity test discriminates FA, possible somatic mosaicism, and non-FA marrow failure at the first diagnostic step, but definitive diagnosis still requires follow-up mutation testing and fibroblast chromosome breakage analysis. [claim:clm_029]
- **Surveillance cadence (RCC).** Given low rates of clonal evolution/PNH, the RCC authors state yearly bone-marrow exams can be omitted, with peripheral blood counts every 3-6 months and marrow study only if counts deviate from baseline. [claim:clm_006]
- **Multidisciplinary escalation.** Given the rarity and management complexity of AA, the guideline strongly recommends multidisciplinary discussion and involvement of expert centres to improve outcomes — a referral/escalation trigger. [claim:clm_067]
- **Proposed multilineage scope exit.** **Speculation:** PROPOSED SCOPE EXIT: a pediatric CBC showing multilineage cytopenia (>=2 of anemia, neutropenia <1.5x10^9/L, thrombocytopenia <150x10^9/L) accompanied by reticulocytopenia should exit the CBC interpretation module and refer to pediatric hematology for marrow evaluation, because acquired aplastic anemia is defined as pancytopenia plus a hypoplastic marrow and a bone-marrow biopsy is stated as mandatory for diagnosis — an implementation boundary the CBC module cannot resolve in-line. [claim:clm_spec01]
- **Proposed malignancy referral gate.** **Speculation:** PROPOSED REFERRAL GATE (marrow infiltration / malignancy): before any inherited-marrow-failure interpretation, a pediatric patient with cytopenia suspected for bone-marrow failure should first be screened for leukemia or other malignant marrow disease and referred to a pediatric oncology center if indicated, mirroring the Atmar protocol's stated first diagnostic step — marrow infiltration is a scope exit that must precede the IBMFS pathway. [claim:clm_spec02]
- **Proposed inherited-syndrome exclusion exit.** **Speculation:** PROPOSED SCOPE EXIT (inherited-syndrome exclusion): acquired AA can be diagnosed only after excluding inherited bone marrow failure syndromes, PNH, connective-tissue disorders, viral infection and drug/heavy-metal suppression, and for patients aged <=40 years (encompassing the entire pediatric band) this entails reflex Fanconi chromosome-breakage and telomere-length testing; because the CBC module cannot perform these exclusions it must refer rather than label an acquired cause. [claim:clm_spec03]
- **Proposed urgent-referral trigger.** **Speculation:** PROPOSED URGENT-REFERRAL TRIGGER: a confirmed very-severe-AA count pattern (neutrophils <0.2x10^9/L within the multilineage-failure picture) should be treated as an urgent (not routine) referral, since it marks the highest-risk severity band and the guideline literature strongly recommends multidisciplinary discussion and expert-centre involvement given AA rarity and management complexity. [claim:clm_spec04]
- **Proposed RCC-vs-AA exit.** **Speculation:** PROPOSED SCOPE EXIT (refractory cytopenia of childhood vs AA indistinguishable on counts): because 84% of RCC present with a hypocellular marrow that is count-indistinguishable from acquired AA, and EWOG-MDS scope-exit to early HSCT hinges on transfusion dependency, ANC <1x10^9/L, monosomy 7, del(7q) or >=2 chromosomal aberrations, a pediatric multilineage-cytopenia module cannot separate RCC from AA on the CBC alone and must refer for marrow morphology plus karyotype. [claim:clm_spec05]

## Conflicts & method-dependence

- **Per-lineage cutoffs diverge by intent (preserve, do not average).** **Inference:** Pediatric per-lineage cytopenia cutoffs diverge by source intent and must be preserved as a conflict: suspected-pediatric-BMF screening uses neutrophils <1.5x10^9/L and platelets <150x10^9/L (Atmar), whereas the BSH acquired-AA diagnostic construct requires >=2 of Hb <100 g/L, platelets <50x10^9/L, neutrophils <1.5x10^9/L; the platelet threshold differs 3-fold (150 vs 50 x10^9/L) and reflects screening-vs-diagnosis intent, not a reconcilable single value. [claim:clm_inf01]
- **Hb targets diverge by disease/context (one carries a unit artifact).** **Inference:** Reported hemoglobin targets diverge across pediatric-relevant sources and must be preserved as context-specific conflicts, never averaged or emitted as a directive: IAP conservative transfusion target Hb >6 g/dL in comorbidity-free children, DBA-syndrome acceptable Hb >=9-10 g/dL independent of age, and the Delphi RBC-transfusion trigger stated verbatim as Hb <7 g/L [sic, a probable g/dL unit error] — each is disease- and context-bound and one carries an unresolved unit artifact. [claim:clm_inf13]
- **Cross-guideline convergence on the Camitta severity cutoffs.** **Inference:** Two independent 2024 guidelines converge on identical modified-Camitta severe-AA scope-exit thresholds: both the all-ages Delphi consensus and the BSH guideline state marrow cellularity <25% (or 25-50% with <30% residual) plus >=2 of neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L, platelets <20x10^9/L, reticulocytes <60x10^9/L for SAA, and neutrophils <0.2x10^9/L for very-severe AA — cross-source agreement raising confidence these are the stable multilineage-marrow-failure severity cutoffs. [claim:clm_inf02]
- **Reticulocyte cutoff is analyzer-dependent.** **Inference:** The severe-AA reticulocyte criterion (<60x10^9/L) is analyzer-method-dependent: BSH states it was modified from a manual percentage to an absolute automated count because automated analyzers over-estimate reticulocytes versus manual counts, so a pediatric CDS applying the <60x10^9/L cutoff must confirm the value is an automated absolute count and must not apply it to a manual-percentage-derived reticulocyte result. [claim:clm_inf03]
- **First-line screens cannot exclude an inherited syndrome.** **Inference:** First-line functional screens for inherited marrow failure are explicitly non-terminal and a CBC-triggered referral must route to a confirmatory tier rather than clear on a single negative blood assay: the MMC chromosome-breakage FA screen requires follow-up mutation testing plus skin-fibroblast breakage analysis to catch somatic-reversion mosaicism, and short-read DNA sequencing missed 2 of 6 SDS cases recovered only by RNA-seq/proteomics. [claim:clm_inf09]
- The Camitta reticulocyte severity criterion has been modified from manual percentage to an absolute count <60 ×10⁹/L when measured by automated analyzers, which over-estimate reticulocytes relative to manual counts. [claim:clm_064]
- Where marrow cellularity is patchy, an overall average cellularity of <30% (excluding lymphocytes and plasma cells) should be ascertained to support the AA diagnosis. [claim:clm_065]
- Six of six SDS patients showed significantly decreased SBDS protein, and two of them were not diagnosed by DNA sequencing alone (monoallelic on short-read DNA-seq, second variant recovered by RNA-seq). [claim:clm_020]
- In 417 samples, the targeted-proteomic SBDS screen had 85.7% sensitivity / 93.4% specificity and the ADH5 screen 100.0% sensitivity / 97.5% specificity for SDS and ADH5/ALDH2 deficiency respectively. [claim:clm_018]
- The SBDS and ADH5 protein-expression cutoffs were defined as the cohort mean minus two standard deviations, computed on the 417 samples excluding confirmed SDS and ADH5/ALDH2 cases. [claim:clm_019]
- The ADH5/ALDH2-deficiency-associated ALDH2 variant (c.1510G>A, p.E504K [rs671]) is a common SNP present in 25.5% of East Asians, so ADH5 proteomics should be paired with genotyping in Asian IBMFS work-ups. [claim:clm_024]
- Erythrocyte adenosine deaminase (eADA) activity helps differentiate DBA syndrome from other anaemias/IBMFS with 84% sensitivity and 95% specificity, but must be measured pre-transfusion and a normal value does not rule out DBA. [claim:clm_043]
- Mean mitomycin C (MMC)-induced chromosome breaks per cell were far higher in FA patients (6.67 ±3.92) than in non-FA patients (0.23 ±0.18). [claim:clm_025]
- The percentage of cells with spontaneous (baseline, non-MMC) chromosome aberrations was more than 9 times higher in FA than in non-FA patients. [claim:clm_028]
- Lymphocyte cultures were incubated for 72 h with MMC at 0, 50, 150, or 300 nM to induce clastogenic breakage. [claim:clm_027]
- Median peripheral-blood NK-cell percentage of total lymphocytes was significantly lower in the SAA group than in the identified-cause (constitutional) group (3.0 vs 9.0; P<0.05). [claim:clm_074]
- The primary WES gene filter (BMF gene panel) contained 172 genes plus HPO-term-driven selection, expanded to PID genes / open-WES / Trio-WES when primary analysis was negative. [claim:clm_075]

## Open questions

- **No pediatric reference intervals located — an explicit gap.** **Inference:** No source in this run supplies age-, sex-, or gestational/postnatal-age-partitioned pediatric reference intervals for defining cytopenia per lineage; every located per-lineage number is an adult AA severity cutoff, a study enrollment criterion, or a disease-specific target, so the module's normal/abnormal boundaries — especially for neonates and infants — are an explicit evidence GAP that must be filled from a dedicated reference-interval source (e.g., CALIPER) before deployment. [claim:clm_inf11]
- **Camitta thresholds unconfirmed in pediatric-scoped verbatim text.** **Inference:** The count-based severe / very-severe AA definitions underpinning the three pediatric-authored aplastic-anemia guidelines in this run (AIEOP, Shimano pediatric recommendations, IAP) could NOT be located in accessible verbatim text, so the asserted Camitta SAA/vSAA numbers rest solely on the adult BSH and all-ages Delphi sources — a pediatric CDS should flag that no pediatric-scoped source verbatim-confirmed these thresholds in this evidence run. [claim:clm_inf12]
- What age-, sex-, and gestational/postnatal-age-partitioned pediatric CBC reference intervals should define per-lineage cytopenia for neonates, infants, and children before deployment?
- Do the pediatric-authored guidelines (AIEOP, Shimano, IAP) state SAA/vSAA count thresholds identical to the adult/all-ages Camitta numbers when their full text is retrieved?
- What pre-test-probability weighting between inherited and acquired marrow failure is appropriate across the pediatric age bands?
